figures.caption: Fig. 4. CDF of time steps required to reach a feasible target service level (when CS-FUB indicates feasibility). Latent policies reach feasible service significantly faster.
Fig. 3. CS-FUB feasibility breakdown showing the fraction of time steps where full, partial, or no user service is achievable under the candidate-set feasibility upper bound.
TABLE I ENVIRONMENT, DATASET, REPRESENTATION, AND TRAINING PARAMETERS.
Fig. 2. Comparison of average served users, peak served users, and discounted (CS-FUB-normalized) performance for latent and raw-state policies.

Fig. 1. CS-FUB illustration. Candidate UAV placements Pt and optimal location p⋆ are shown. Served and unserved users are indicated in green and red, respectively. The dashed blue contour denotes the coverage boundary (e.g., −90 dBm), highlighting geometry- and propagation-induced limits.
